description: Close critical unit test coverage gaps across the platform. Add tests for untested pure functions, export trapped utility functions for testability, and add tests for core business logic with missing coverage. All changes are unit test additions — no production behavior changes.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for parseManifest in manifest-schema.ts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want unit tests for the parseManifest function so that manifest validation regressions are caught before they reach E2E tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover: valid minimal manifest (name, version, description, url_patterns with 1 entry, tools with 1 valid tool)
Tests cover: invalid JSON input throws with 'Invalid JSON in manifest' message
Tests cover: missing required field 'name' throws with path-specific error
Tests cover: missing required field 'version' throws with path-specific error
Tests cover: missing required field 'description' throws with path-specific error
Tests cover: empty url_patterns array throws with 'At least one URL pattern' message
Tests cover: empty tools array throws with 'At least one tool' message
Tests cover: tool missing name field throws with path like 'tools.0.name'
Tests cover: tool missing description field throws with path like 'tools.0.description'
Tests cover: tool missing input_schema throws
Tests cover: tool missing output_schema throws
Tests cover: extra fields (e.g. adapterHash, displayName, custom fields) pass through without error
Tests cover: valid manifest with optional displayName returns it in the result
Tests cover: valid manifest with optional adapterHash returns it in the result
userStories.notes: parseManifest is at platform/mcp-server/src/manifest-schema.ts:37-52. It is a pure function: takes a raw JSON string and a sourcePath string, returns PluginManifest or throws. Zero mocking needed. The Zod schema (pluginManifestSchema) uses z.looseObject so extra fields are allowed. Follow the test style of existing files like state.test.ts — use describe/test blocks with clear names. Import parseManifest directly. For error tests, use expect(() => parseManifest(input, '/test/path')).toThrow(/expected message/). The sourcePath is included in error messages so test it appears in the thrown error string.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for isConnectionRefused in CLI config.ts
userStories.description: As a CLI developer, I want unit tests for isConnectionRefused so that connection error detection logic used by 3 CLI commands is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests added to platform/cli/src/config.test.ts in a new describe('isConnectionRefused') block
Tests cover: TypeError with cause.code === 'ECONNREFUSED' returns true
Tests cover: TypeError without cause property returns false
Tests cover: TypeError with cause but different code (e.g. 'ENOTFOUND') returns false
Tests cover: non-TypeError Error returns false
Tests cover: plain string value returns false
userStories.notes: isConnectionRefused is at platform/cli/src/config.ts:42-46. It is already exported. The function checks: (1) instanceof TypeError, (2) .cause?.code === 'ECONNREFUSED'. To create test TypeError with cause: `const err = new TypeError('fetch failed'); Object.defineProperty(err, 'cause', { value: { code: 'ECONNREFUSED' } })`. Or use: `const err = new TypeError('fetch failed', { cause: { code: 'ECONNREFUSED' } })` if the TypeError constructor supports it. Add tests to the existing config.test.ts file — it already imports from '../config.js'.

userStories.title: Export and test pure utility functions from CLI status.ts
userStories.description: As a CLI developer, I want formatUptime, colorTabState, and isTimeout exported and tested so that formatting logic is verified and reusable.
acceptanceCriteria: formatUptime, colorTabState, and isTimeout are exported from platform/cli/src/commands/status.ts

formatUptime tests cover: 0 seconds, 30 seconds, 59 seconds (boundary before minutes), 60 seconds, 90 seconds, 3599 seconds (boundary before hours), 3600 seconds, 7200 seconds, 86399 seconds (boundary before days), 86400 seconds, multi-day values
colorTabState tests cover: 'ready' input, 'unavailable' input, 'closed' input, unknown string input
isTimeout tests cover: DOMException with name 'TimeoutError' returns true, DOMException with different name returns false, regular Error returns false, non-Error value returns false
No knip unused export warnings for the newly exported functions
userStories.notes: The functions are at platform/cli/src/commands/status.ts. formatUptime (lines ~18-28) has 4 branches: <60 → seconds, <3600 → minutes, <86400 → hours, else → days. colorTabState (lines ~30-33) returns picocolors-wrapped strings: green for 'ready', yellow for 'unavailable', dim for anything else. isTimeout (lines ~35-38) checks `err instanceof DOMException && err.name === 'TimeoutError'`. To export them, add them to an export statement at the bottom of the file or add 'export' keyword to each const declaration. For the test file, follow the pattern in platform/cli/src/parse-port.test.ts. Note: colorTabState returns picocolors-wrapped strings — test that the output contains the input string (not exact match on ANSI codes). For isTimeout, DOMException is available globally in Bun — create with `new DOMException('timeout', 'TimeoutError')`. Also create the tsconfig.test.json for this package's commands directory if needed — check if platform/cli/tsconfig.test.json already covers src/**/*.test.ts (it does, so no new tsconfig needed).

userStories.title: Export and test pure utility functions from CLI doctor.ts
userStories.description: As a CLI developer, I want checkExtensionConnected and checkBunVersion exported and tested so that diagnostic check logic is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: checkExtensionConnected and checkBunVersion are exported from platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts
checkExtensionConnected tests cover: null health data returns warn result, connected=true returns pass result, connected=false returns fail result
checkBunVersion tests cover: returns a pass result with current Bun version in detail
All test assertions verify the result shape has status ('pass', 'warn', or 'fail'), label, and detail fields
No knip unused export warnings for the newly exported functions
userStories.notes: The functions are in platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts. checkExtensionConnected takes a `data` parameter (health endpoint response object or null) and checks `data?.extensionConnected`. checkBunVersion reads `Bun.version`. The CheckResult type is an object with `{ status: 'pass'|'warn'|'fail', label: string, detail: string, hint?: string }` — it's defined locally in doctor.ts. To export the functions, add 'export' to their const declarations. You may also need to export the CheckResult type if the test file needs it for type annotations. Follow the test pattern in platform/cli/src/config.test.ts.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for determineTrustTier in discovery.ts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want unit tests for the trust tier classification logic so that plugin security boundaries are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests added to platform/mcp-server/src/discovery.test.ts in a new describe('determineTrustTier') block
Tests cover: isLocal=true with any pkgName returns 'local'
Tests cover: isLocal=true with null pkgName returns 'local'
Tests cover: isLocal=false with null pkgName returns 'community'
userStories.notes: determineTrustTier is at platform/mcp-server/src/discovery.ts:59-63. It's a pure function: `(pkgName: string | null, isLocal: boolean) => TrustTier`. Three branches: isLocal → 'local', @opentabs-dev/ prefix → 'official', else → 'community'. To export it, change `const determineTrustTier` to `export const determineTrustTier` or add it to the export list at the bottom of the file. Add tests to the existing discovery.test.ts which already tests pluginNameFromPackage.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for reloadExtension browser tool handler
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want unit tests for the reloadExtension handler so that all 3 code paths (no ws, send success, send failure) are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover: extensionWs is null/undefined returns { ok: false, error: 'Extension not connected' }
Tests cover: extensionWs.send succeeds returns { ok: true, message containing 'Reload signal sent' }
Tests cover: extensionWs.send throws returns { ok: false, error containing 'Failed to send' }
Tests verify the JSON-RPC message sent contains method 'extension.reload' and a numeric id
userStories.notes: reloadExtension is at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/reload-extension.ts. The handler accesses `state.extensionWs` and calls `getNextRequestId(state)`. To test, import the reloadExtension object and call its handler directly. The handler signature is `(args, state) => Promise<result>`. Mock state as `{ extensionWs: { send: vi.fn() }, nextRequestId: 1 }` — but since this uses bun:test not vitest, use `mock()` from bun:test or just create plain objects. For the send-throws case: `state.extensionWs = { send: () => { throw new Error('ws closed') } }`. You'll need to also handle `getNextRequestId` — it reads `state.nextRequestId` and increments it. Check how state.test.ts creates state objects for the pattern. Note: the handler is on the `reloadExtension` object, not a standalone function — check the `defineBrowserTool` return shape to find how to access it. Read platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/definition.ts to understand the BrowserToolDefinition shape — the handler is likely at `reloadExtension.handler`.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for bridge setAllToolsEnabled and request timeout
userStories.description: As an extension developer, I want setAllToolsEnabled tested and the 30s request timeout path tested so that side panel reliability is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Test verifies setAllToolsEnabled sends correct JSON-RPC message with method 'config.setAllToolsEnabled' and params { plugin, enabled }
Test verifies request timeout: a request that never gets a response rejects after REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS with a timeout error message
Test verifies that after timeout, the pending request is cleaned up from the internal map
userStories.notes: bridge.ts is at platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts. setAllToolsEnabled is exported and calls sendRequest('config.setAllToolsEnabled', { plugin, enabled }). For the timeout test: sendRequest sets a setTimeout of REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS (30000ms). To test without waiting 30s, you can either: (1) use Bun's fake timer support if available, or (2) temporarily reduce the timeout for testing. The preferred approach is to spy on the chrome.runtime.sendMessage mock to capture the sent message and verify its shape, then never call handleServerResponse — wait for the timeout. Since the existing tests in bridge.test.ts already mock chrome.runtime.sendMessage, follow the same pattern. For the timeout, you may need to advance timers — check if bun:test supports `jest.useFakeTimers()` or similar. If not, the test can set a shorter timeout by importing and modifying REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS if it's exported, or the test can accept a longer runtime.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for findAllMatchingTabs ranking logic
userStories.description: As an extension developer, I want unit tests for the tab ranking logic so that tool dispatch selects the correct tab when multiple tabs match.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests added to platform/browser-extension/src/tab-matching.test.ts in a new describe('findAllMatchingTabs') block
Tests verify: active+focused tab is ranked first
Tests verify: active-only tab is ranked above focused-only tab
Tests verify: inactive unfocused tab is ranked last
Tests verify: deduplication when same tab matches multiple URL patterns
Tests verify: empty result when no tabs match
userStories.notes: findAllMatchingTabs is at platform/browser-extension/src/tab-matching.ts:93-134. It calls chrome.tabs.query for each URL pattern, deduplicates by tab ID, then ranks by a score function: active && windowId===focusedWindowId → 3, active → 2, windowId===focusedWindowId → 1, else → 0. Sorted descending. To test, mock chrome.tabs.query to return arrays of tab objects ({id, url, active, windowId}) and chrome.windows.getLastFocused to return {id: focusedWindowId}. The existing test file already has Chrome API mocking infrastructure from plugin-storage.test.ts — check how it mocks chrome.tabs. The tab-matching.test.ts file currently doesn't mock Chrome APIs since it only tests pure functions. You'll need to set up globalThis.chrome = { tabs: { query: mock }, windows: { getLastFocused: mock } } in a beforeEach.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN in shared
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want unit tests for the extension copy exclude regex so that the extension install copy filter is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests added to platform/shared/src/validation.test.ts in a new describe('EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN') block
Tests verify pattern does NOT match: 'dist', 'dist/background.js', 'manifest.json', 'icons', 'icons/icon-128.png', 'side-panel', 'side-panel/index.html'
userStories.notes: EXTENSION_COPY_EXCLUDE_PATTERN is exported from platform/shared/src/index.ts. It's a RegExp constant. The tests should call pattern.test(path) with various relative paths. Read the actual regex to understand what it matches — it likely tests against the relative path from the source directory. The validation.test.ts file already imports from the shared package, so just add a new describe block. Check the exact regex value before writing tests to ensure the test expectations match the actual pattern.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for executeScript browser tool cleanup contract
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want unit tests verifying that deleteExecFile is always called in the finally block of the executeScript handler.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests mock writeExecFile, dispatchToExtension, and deleteExecFile
Tests verify: on successful dispatch, deleteExecFile is called with the filename from writeExecFile
Tests verify: when dispatchToExtension throws, deleteExecFile is still called (finally contract)
Tests verify: the filename from writeExecFile is passed as execFile param to dispatchToExtension
userStories.notes: executeScript is at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/execute-script.ts:18-54. The handler calls writeExecFile(execId, code) which returns a filename, then dispatchToExtension(state, 'browser.executeScript', { tabId, execFile: filename }), with deleteExecFile(filename) in a finally block. To test, use bun:test's module mocking to mock '../extension-protocol.js' — mock writeExecFile to return a predictable filename, mock dispatchToExtension to either resolve or reject, mock deleteExecFile to track calls. Also mock crypto.randomUUID if needed. Access the handler from the exported executeScript object — check the defineBrowserTool return shape (read platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/definition.ts). The handler is likely at executeScript.handler. Pass a mock state object similar to the reload-extension tests.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for handleServerMessage routing in message-router.ts
userStories.description: As an extension developer, I want unit tests for the handleServerMessage function so that the central server-to-extension message routing is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests added to platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.test.ts in a new describe('handleServerMessage') block
Tests mock all handler dependencies: plugin-storage, iife-injection, tab-state, tool-dispatch, browser-commands, messaging
Tests verify: sync.full message calls handleSyncFull (validate plugins, store, inject, sync tab state)
Tests verify: plugin.update message calls handlePluginUpdate with validated payload
Tests verify: plugin.uninstall message calls handlePluginUninstall with cleanup
Tests verify: tool.dispatch message delegates to handleToolDispatch
Tests verify: unrecognized method with id sends -32601 error response
Tests verify: side panel method forwarding (tab.stateChanged, tool.invocationStart, etc.) calls forwardToSidePanel
Tests verify: non-side-panel methods (sync.full) do NOT call forwardToSidePanel
userStories.notes: handleServerMessage is at platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts:380-414. It's the main routing function that dispatches based on the `method` field. The function uses a `methodHandlers` map (lines 123-216) and a `SIDE_PANEL_METHODS` set (lines 25-30). To test, you need to mock: (1) plugin-storage.ts (storePluginsBatch, removePlugin, etc.), (2) iife-injection.ts (injectPluginIntoMatchingTabs, cleanupAdaptersInMatchingTabs), (3) tab-state.ts (sendTabSyncAll, computePluginTabState, clearPluginTabState), (4) tool-dispatch.ts (handleToolDispatch), (5) browser-commands.ts (handleBrowser*), (6) messaging.ts (sendToServer, forwardToSidePanel). Use bun:test's module mocking. The function receives the raw parsed message object. For sync.full, test with a minimal valid payload: { method: 'sync.full', params: { plugins: [...] } }. For unrecognized method with id, test with { method: 'unknown.method', id: 1 } and verify sendToServer is called with error response.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for loadPluginFromDir in discovery.ts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want unit tests for the plugin loading function so that manifest validation, IIFE size limits, and name derivation are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests added to platform/mcp-server/src/discovery.test.ts in a new describe('loadPluginFromDir') block
Tests use real temp directories with real files (same pattern as reload.test.ts)
Tests cover: valid plugin with manifest and IIFE loads correctly with all fields populated
Tests cover: missing IIFE file throws with 'not found' message
Tests cover: empty IIFE file throws with 'empty' message
Tests cover: invalid plugin name in manifest throws
Tests cover: local plugin (npmPkgName=null) derives name from manifest.name
Tests cover: npm plugin derives name from package name via pluginNameFromPackage
Tests cover: manifest name mismatch with npm package name logs warning but does not throw
userStories.notes: loadPluginFromDir is at platform/mcp-server/src/discovery.ts:69-144. To export it, add it to the export list at the bottom of the file. The function reads from disk, so tests should use temp directories (follow the pattern in reload.test.ts which creates temp dirs with mkdtempSync). Create a minimal valid plugin: write opentabs-plugin.json with {name, version, description, url_patterns, tools} and dist/adapter.iife.js with any non-empty content. For the IIFE size test, skip the 5MB test since creating a 5MB temp file is slow — focus on the other validation paths. The function takes (dir, trustTier, npmPkgName, sourcePath?). For local plugins, pass npmPkgName=null. For npm plugins, pass the package name string. The name mismatch warning calls log.warn — you may need to spy on the logger to verify this.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for scaffoldPlugin domain-to-URL-pattern conversion
userStories.description: As a CLI developer, I want unit tests for the domain-to-URL-pattern conversion logic in scaffoldPlugin and for toPascalCase so that plugin scaffolding correctness is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: scaffoldPlugin tests use real temp directories and verify: valid scaffold creates expected file structure (package.json, src/index.ts, src/tools/example-tool.ts, tsconfig.json, .prettierrc)
scaffoldPlugin tests verify: domain 'slack.com' produces URL pattern '*://slack.com/*'
scaffoldPlugin tests verify: domain '.slack.com' produces URL pattern '*://*.slack.com/*'
scaffoldPlugin tests verify: invalid name throws ScaffoldError
scaffoldPlugin tests verify: existing directory throws ScaffoldError
userStories.notes: scaffold.ts is at platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts. toPascalCase is currently not exported — add 'export' to its declaration. scaffoldPlugin is the main export. For integration tests, create a temp directory (mkdtempSync), call scaffoldPlugin with valid args pointing to a subdirectory of the temp dir, then verify the file structure exists. The domain-to-pattern conversion is at the point where args.domain is processed — look for `.startsWith('.')` logic. For the existing directory test, create the target directory before calling scaffoldPlugin. For the invalid name test, pass a name that fails validatePluginName (e.g., 'My Plugin' with space). The ScaffoldError class is also exported from scaffold.ts. Note: scaffoldPlugin calls autoRegisterPlugin which reads/writes config.json — you'll need to set OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR env var to a temp directory to isolate it, or handle the registration step in tests.

userStories.title: Add edge case tests for validatePlugin tool name starting with digit
userStories.description: As a CLI developer, I want the digit-at-start constraint for tool names documented by a test case.
acceptanceCriteria: Test added to platform/cli/src/commands/build.test.ts verifying tool name '1tool' produces a validation error
Test added verifying tool with both empty name and empty description produces two errors
userStories.notes: build.test.ts is at platform/cli/src/commands/build.test.ts. Add tests in the existing 'tool validation' describe block. The tool name regex requires ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ so '1tool' fails. For the multi-error test, create a tool with name: '' and description: '' and verify errors.length >= 2. Follow the exact test pattern used by adjacent tests in the file.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for sendAllToolsEnabled alias and bridge cleanup
userStories.description: As an extension developer, I want the bridge.ts setAllToolsEnabled function tested separately to verify its method name and params shape.
acceptanceCriteria: Test added to platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.test.ts verifying setAllToolsEnabled sends JSON-RPC with method 'config.setAllToolsEnabled'
Test verifies the params include { plugin: string, enabled: boolean }
userStories.notes: setAllToolsEnabled is at platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts. It calls sendRequest('config.setAllToolsEnabled', { plugin, enabled }). Follow the exact pattern of the existing setToolEnabled test in bridge.test.ts — it captures the message sent via chrome.runtime.sendMessage and verifies the method and params fields. The test should be in the existing bridge.test.ts file, added near the setToolEnabled tests.
